sections.paragraph:
συγκρατέω fut. συγκρατήσω; 1 aor. pass. συνεκρατήθην (Plut. et al.; Ps 16:5 Sym.; Jos., Ant. 8, 67; Ath. 10, 1)

① to keep parts together, hold together w. acc. (Anaximenes [VI b.c.] 2 Diels: ἡ ψυχὴ συγκρατεῖ ἡμᾶς) Hs 9, 7, 5.

② to be supportive by surrounding, surround (and protect) τὸν λαόν Hs 5, 5, 3; cp. 9, 12, 8.

③ to give suppport to, support, hold upright (cp. Aretaeus 3, 5, 7; 40, 29 Hude ὕπνος συγκ. τὰ μέλεα; Geopon., Prooem. 6) pass., of a sick pers. ἵνα συγκρατηθῇ ἡ ἀσθένεια τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ that the person’s weak body might find support Hv 3, 11, 4.—DELG s.v. κράτος.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aretaeus Aretaeus , II a.d.—List 5

Geopon Geopon , a collection of older material, X a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 953
